title: CI: trace setup and runner scripts
body:
Every few months we get a new CI failure that requires
scanning through logs that give no indication of what
is happening or where. Tracking down the error can
cost many hours.

Solution: pepper setup & runner scripts with showrun(), which
echoes the command _and_ displays the source filename + lineno.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Oh, this is soooo nice. Why didn't I do this sooner? Even something just [as simple as](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5247452689727488?logs=main#L32):
```
+ make validate  # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/./contrib/cirrus/runner.sh:32 in _run_validate()
```
...showing what script is running, what the call stack looks like, and **the command being run**, is going to be such a comfort next time we get a bizarre CI failure.

@cevich PTAL when convenient. No rush. FWIW I've hand-examined a number of Cirrus logs, but not every individual one.
I didn't realize `lib.sh` was in this repo, I thought it was in another one. I've instrumented that one too, thank you!

Quick breeze review LGTM

Some tests are not happy, I'm not sure if that is expected.
